observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The Municipal Accountant and the GSO Head to effect the necessary adjustments and update their respective records; b. The Inventory Committee to finalize the Physical Inventory Plan and the target date of the one-time cleansing; and c. The Inventory Committee to proceed with the conduct of the one-time cleansing in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 as soon as possible.
observations.reason: One-time cleansing has not yet been implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Reclassify or transfer the subject PPEs to Investment Property account; and b. Ensure that all disclosures on Investment Property are incorporated in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Disclosure required by IPSAS 16 was lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The Acting-Municipal Treasurer to: i. Ensure accuracy and completeness in the preparation of the Report of Checks Issued following Sections 43 and 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; ii. Prepare and provide a copy of the list of unreleased checks to the OIC-Municipal Accountant at the end of the year; iii. Send written notices to the concerned payees one month before the check becomes stale; and iv. Report and attach stale checks to the RCI prepared during the period of cancellation. b. The OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments for unreleased checks at year-end to correct the Cash in Bank and corresponding Payable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: No adjustments were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The concerned implementing offices to expedite the implementation of the PPAs to utilize the fund transfers within the prescribed period, and observe strictly the parameters set in the Memorandum of Agreement; b. The OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure prompt liquidation of fund transfers from NGAs upon completion of funded PPAs, and record the same in the books accordingly; c. The OIC-Municipal Accountant to exhaust efforts in retrieving the supporting documents and expediting the return of the unexpended balances to the Source Agencies; d. The OIC-Municipal Accountant to send confirmation letters to the Source Agencies to validate the balance per books of the prior years’ unutilized fund transfers; and e. The Acting Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the concerned implementing offices upon receipt of fund transfers from various NGAs for the timely implementation by the said offices of the PPAs.
observations.reason: Ongoing fund transfers were not yet liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Direct the GSO Head to prepare the request for relief from accountability and its supporting documents, and submit the same to the COA Director as soon as possible to relieve him and the Municipality from the liability for the damaged property; b. Require the GSO Head to determine the recoverable service amount of the fire-damaged Residual Containment Area so that the appropriate impairment loss, if any, will be recognized in the books of accounts; c. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the impairment loss, if any, on the damaged asset and provide the adjusted depreciation allowance and necessary accounting disclosures following IPSAS 21; and d. Direct the concerned personnel to verify if there is any MOA that supports the transfer of the Rainwater-Harvesting Facility, and get a copy thereof from the Iloilo Provincial Government for accounting and documentary purposes.
observations.reason: Accountability is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned departments/offices to: a. Be specific in the identification of priority PPAs to be charged against the 20% DF such that the PPAs are properly included in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and that the details and costings thereof are sufficiently contained in the Annual Budget; and b. Ensure that approved Sangguniang Bayan resolutions are secured prior to processing of payments for PPAs with lumpsum allocation, if any. We also recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to obligate the Continuing Appropriation of the 20% DF primarily before obligating the Current Appropriation in view of propriety and rationality.
observations.reason: Several PPAs were not specified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Direct the Inventory Committee to cause the performance of periodic physical inventory of supplies pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. The discrepancy between the inventory report and the accounting records should be investigated, reconciled, and adjusted; b. Require the preparation and submission of the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories. This shall be submitted to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and c. Instruct the GSO Head to prepare and submit regularly the monthly fuel consumption reports to the Accounting Office and the Auditor so that expenses on fuel, oil, and lubricants could be taken up in the books in a timely manner following the principle of matching, and that the economical and efficient use of said petroleum products is properly safeguarded.
observations.reason: Only fuel consumption reports, which were used to account for Fuel, Oil, and Lubricants inventory and expenses, were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned personnel to exhaust measures in expediting the processing of titling and make further research necessary for the retrieval of land titles and preparation of pertinent documents for the transfer certificates of title. We further recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments and reflect the accurate balance of the Municipality’s Land account.
observations.reason: Titling of other land properties, however, is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the installation of a CCTV camera inside the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to ensure regularity of transactions and detect and deter possible misuse of cash.
observations.reason: No CCTV was installed inside the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office and devise a more systematic way of processing the GPPA documents so that the reports related thereto can be prepared accurately and submitted on time.
observations.reason: Report on GPPAs was not submitted for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the Officers and Employees of the Municipality of Tubungan to refund the monetary incentive given as the requirements for the grant of monetary awards were not met, hence resulting in an illegal expenditure.
observations.reason: Unrefunded balance is from retired/separated employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the refund of the excess amount of PEI for CY 2014 totaling ₱400,000.00 paid to officials and personnel.
observations.reason: Outstanding balance of ₱77,000.00 is still for refund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the refund of the excess amount of PEI for CY 2013 totaling ₱1,200,000.00 paid to officials and personnel.
observations.reason: The amount to be refunded was partially collected through salary deductions.

observations.recommendation:
1.9. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Inventory Committee to exhaust efforts and measures to conduct a
complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s assets and submit the
RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year in order for the
accuracy and existence of PPE accounts to be verified in a timely
manner.

1.10. We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Inventory Committee to consider conducting the one-time cleansing of
the PPE accounts as soon as the complete physical count of PPE is
undertaken, and knowledge on the cleansing process is sufficiently
gathered.

1.11. We further recommended and the Inventory Committee and other
concerned personnel agreed to refer to COA Circular No. 2020-006 for
the guidelines and procedures of the one-time cleansing, and note the
areas that concern and hinder the Municipality from conducting such
activity.

2.11. We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to
prepare adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts pertinent to
the received projects from DPWH.

2.12. We also recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to
record henceforth the assets received from other agencies properly by
recording the same at net carrying value.

3.11. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reclassify the completed projects from Construction in Progress to
appropriate Property, Plant, and Equipment accounts, and provide the
corresponding allowance for depreciation in compliance with IPSAS 17; and
b. Ensure that Certificates of Completion are attached or submitted
completely to the Accounting Office by the Engineering Office upon
processing of final billing or payments to contractors.

4.11. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical
inventory of supplies pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual
for LGUs, Volume I;
b. Require the preparation of the Report of the Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) and its submission to the Audit Team not later
than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semesters, respectively, as well as to the Municipal Accountant for
reconciliation with the accounting records;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the perpetual
inventory records of Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) for each
commodity/stock, and the General Services Office (GSO) to likewise
maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies;
d. Instruct the GSO Head to submit the fuel consumption reports on a
monthly basis or, at the very least, ensure that fuel consumption
reports for the calendar year are forwarded to the Accounting
Office before the end of the year; and
e. Instruct the HRMO and GSO Heads to review the manning
complement of the GSO and assess the need for additional
manpower to address the reporting deficiencies thereof.

5.7 We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Include prospectively in the Notes to Financial Statements
disclosures for the Municipality’s Road Network System using the
template provided in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
b. Coordinate with the Engineering Office and/or GSO for the
necessary data to be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

6.11. We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to record
the monetization of leave credits of all personnel, resigned or
incumbent, under Terminal Leave Benefits account.

6.12. We also recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to
take up the accrual of Leave Benefits Payable upon determination of
the cost or money value of the accumulated leave credits to be
monetized for the year.

6.13. We further recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed
to disclose in the financial statements the details and financial impact
of the impending full and retroactive recognition of the cost or money
value of the accumulated leave credits of all municipal personnel whilst
awaiting clear budgeting guidelines thereon.

7.10. We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to
coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in determining the proper
account codes to be used for both budgeting and accounting purposes
based on the nature of the expense items to ensure consistency and
accuracy, and to avoid the indiscriminate use of Other Maintenance and
Operating Expense account.

7.11. We also recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to
record accounts and transactions based on their nature and in accordance
with the RCA for LGUs, and not solely rely on the account codes indicated
in Obligation Requests.

8.8 We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to record
receivable for fund transfers between the GF and the TF based on the
nature thereof and in accordance with the account descriptions
provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a. Review further the previous year’s charges against the Special Trust Fund and make the appropriate adjustments; and b. Establish actual existence and consumption of inventoriable supplies and materials charged against the STF to account for the corresponding assets that need to be transferred to the General Fund; and c. Draw the necessary journal entries to recognize the transfer of assets to the General Fund.

We recommended and the Tubungan Public Market Board (TPMB), headed by the Local Chief Executive, agreed to: a. Develop plans, programs, and strategies to improve the occupancy rate of market stalls; and b. Discuss with the delinquent stall owner the ongoing concern regarding the unpaid liability and take the best possible action in order to minimize the consequence of the deficiency noted. We also recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the cost of materials and labor totaling ₱1.412 million as Investment Property – Market.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Local Finance Committee to prepare quarterly reports on the utilization of the LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC and status of project implementation, and post the same in at least three conspicuous places in the locality and on the LGU’s website within 20 calendar days from the end of each quarter.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to exhaust measures in reconciling the Municipality’ record with that of the Iloilo Provincial Government and in determining the causes of the discrepancies. Upon reconciliation, proper adjustments should be made, if warranted.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Direct the Inventory Committee to cause the performance of periodic physical inventory of supplies pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. The discrepancy between the inventory report and the accounting records should be investigated, reconciled, and adjusted; 



Require the preparation and submission of the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories. This shall be submitted to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and

Instruct the GSO Head to prepare and submit regularly the monthly fuel consumption reports to the Accounting Office and the Auditor so that expenses on fuel, oil, and lubricants could be taken up in the books in a timely manner following the principle of matching, and that the economical and efficient use of said petroleum products is properly safeguarded.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned personnel to exhaust measures in expediting the processing of titling and special patent applications, and make further research necessary for the retrieval of land titles and preparation of pertinent documents for the transfer certificates of title.



We further recommended and Local Chief Executive agreed to require the OIC – Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments and reflect the accurate balance of the Municipality’s Land account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to create an Inventory Committee for the sole purpose of conducting the one-time cleansing of the Municipality’s PPE and direct the said committee to cause the eventual reconciliation of the PPE balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 as soon as possible.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day, if proven feasible and cost-effective. If not, deposits should be done at least once a week but make sure that the amounts for deposit, which must be safekept inside the cash/treasury vault at all times, include all collections made from the last date of deposit to the date preceding the next deposit. 

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to set a cut-off time (e.g., 3:00 P.M.) for the daily remittances from the collectors/tellers and ensure that collections within the cut-off are remitted within the day, and those collected beyond the cut-off are remitted the following working day. 
 
We further recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the installation of a CCTV camera inside the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to ensure regularity of transactions and detect and deter possible misuse of cash.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant to exhaust measures in reconciling the Municipality’ record with that of the Iloilo Provincial Government and in determining the causes of the discrepancies. Upon reconciliation, proper adjustments should be made, if warranted.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office and devise a more systematic way of processing the GPPA documents so that the reports related thereto can be prepared accurately and submitted on time.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Officers and Employees of the Municipality of Tubungan to refund the monetary incentive given as the requirements for the grant of monetary awards were not met, hence resulting in an illegal expenditure.
We recommended the refund of the excess amount of PEI for         CY 2014 totaling ₱400,000.00 paid to officials and personnel.
We recommended the refund of the excess amount of PEI for CY 2013 totaling ₱1,200,000.00 paid to officials and personnel.

1.5.	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant and the GSO Head to effect the necessary adjustments and update their respective records;
The Inventory Committee to finalize the Physical Inventory Plan and the target date of the one-time cleansing; and
The Inventory Committee to proceed with the conduct of the one-time cleansing in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 as soon as possible.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify or transfer the subject PPEs to Investment Property account; and
Ensure that all disclosures on Investment Property are incorporated in the Notes to Financial Statements.

3.11. 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
Ensure accuracy and completeness in the preparation of the Report of Checks Issued following Sections 43 and 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs;
Prepare and provide a copy of the list of unreleased checks to the OIC – Municipal Accountant at the end of the year.
Send written notices to the concerned payees one month before the check becomes stale; and
Report and attach stale checks to the RCI prepared during the period of cancellation.
The OIC – Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments for unreleased checks at year-end to correct the Cash in Bank and corresponding Payable accounts in the financial statements.

5.7. 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the OIC – Municipal  Accountant to:
Review further the previous year’s charges against the Special Trust Fund and make the appropriate accounting adjustments; and
Establish actual existence and consumption of inventoriable supplies and materials charged against the STF to account for the corresponding assets that need to be transferred to the General Fund; and
Draw the necessary journal entries to recognize the transfer of assets to the General Fund.

6.15.  	We recommended and the Tubungan Public Market Board (TPMB), headed by the Local Chief Executive, agreed to:
Develop plans, programs, and strategies to improve the occupancy rate of market stalls; and
Discuss with the delinquent stall owner the ongoing concern regarding the unpaid liability and take the best possible action in order to minimize the consequence of the deficiency noted.
6.16.   	We also recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the cost of materials and labor totaling ₱1.412 million as Investment Property – Market.

8.10.  	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned departments/offices to:
Be specific in the identification of priority PPAs to be charged against the 20% DF such that the PPAs are properly included in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and that the details and costings thereof are sufficiently contained in the Annual Budget; and
Ensure that approved Sangguniang Bayan resolutions are secured prior to processing of payments for PPAs with lumpsum allocation, if any.
We also recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to obligate the Continuing Appropriation of the 20% DF primarily before obligating the Current Appropriation in view of propriety and rationality.

9.7. 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Local Finance Committee to prepare quarterly reports on the utilization of the LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC and status of project implementation, and post the same in at least three conspicuous places in the locality and on the LGU’s website within 20 calendar days from the end of each quarter.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure prospectively that the financial statements properly present the current and non-current portions of loans payable, and accrue the corresponding interest expense as at December 31 of the current year.

We recommended and the Municipal Health Officer agreed to facilitate the submission of the quarterly summary/list of COVID-19 vaccines and other aids and donations received, and its supporting documents within 10 working days after the end of each quarter.

We also recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose prospectively in-kind donations received in the Notes to the Financial Statements, and record the cost of the remaining balance of said donations in the books in the event the quarantine or pandemic ends.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and other concerned municipal heads and personnel agreed to prioritize the finalization of the updating of the Municipality’s local revenue code, pursuant to Section 191 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstated accounts.

We also recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to record prospectively the collections and disbursements for tourism-related PPAs to its proper income and expense accounts, respectively.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the current OIC – MDRRMO to monitor properly the implementation of all projects charged against the LDRRMF, in coordination with other concerned offices, so that benefits that could be derived therefrom would be realized as early as possible.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned departments/offices to collate, update, and make readily available for verification information pertinent to the 20% DF projects as well as all other municipal projects for transparency and convenient monitoring of their completion/implementa-tion.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant to:

Implement the prompt and proper liquidation of funds received from Source Agencies, including the return of excess/unutilized amount.

Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers with the Source Agencies and prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to, through the Sangguniang Bayan, hire additional qualified personnel to occupy the two staff positions to assist the LDRRMO following the position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6 Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121 and Item 5.2, 5.3 and 5.4 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014, to be able to perform their functions and responsibilities properly as LDRRM Officer and staff.

Recommendations

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:


a. Direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant  and  the  Inventory  Committee  to
   cause the reconciliation of the results of actual physical count with the
   property and accounting records pursuant to the pertinent  provisions  of
   the COA Circular
   No. 2020-006. The  discrepancy  between  the  inventory  report  and  the
   accounting records should be investigated, reconciled, and adjusted;


b. Require the preparation and submission of the  Inventory  and  Inspection
   Report of Unserviceable Property to report all unserviceable property for
   disposal;


c. Direct the  concerned  personnel  to  exhaust  efforts  in  locating  the
   missing properties  and  secure  documents  evidencing  the  disposal  of
   properties to effect the dropping and  derecognition  of  these  disposed
   properties from the books of accounts; and


d. Direct the concerned personnel to  verify  the  status  of  the  breeding
   stocks and determine the extent of liability of the  persons  responsible
   for the said assets.


The aforementioned audit observation  was  communicated  to  the  Management
thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-08 dated May 20, 2022.

Recommendation

We recommended  and  the  OIC  –  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to  provide
appropriately  the  corresponding  allowance  for   depreciation   for   the
depreciable components of the Road Networks in compliance with COA  Circular
No. 2015-008.

The aforementioned audit observation  was  communicated  to  the  Management
thru
Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-04 dated April 18, 2022.

Recommendation

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed  to  direct  the  OIC  –
Municipal Accountant to exhaust measures in  reconciling  the  Municipality’
record with that of the Iloilo Provincial Government and in determining  the
causes of the discrepancies. Upon reconciliation, proper adjustments  should
be made, if warranted.

The aforementioned audit observation  was  communicated  to  the  Management
thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-06 dated May 16, 2022.

Recommendations

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the  concerned
departments/offices to:


a. Plan effectively the priority development projects to be included in  the
   AIP by considering their feasibility and necessity;



b. Ensure optimal utilization of the 20% DF  through  proper  prioritization
   and fast-tracking  of  the  implementation  of  development  projects  to
   achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes  for  the
   benefit of the constituents; and


c. Collate, update, and make readily available for verification  information
   pertinent to the 20% DF projects as well as all other municipal  projects
   for    transparency    and     convenient     monitoring     of     their
   completion/implementation.


The aforementioned audit observation  was  communicated  to  the  Management
thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-07 dated May 16, 2022.

Recommendations

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed  to  direct  the  OIC  –
LDRRM Officer and Municipal Budget Officer to:


a. Submit a written explanation on the charging of regular MOOE against  the
   LDRRMF, subject to the Audit Team’s further evaluation; and


b. Discontinue use and allocation of LDRRMF for  any  regular  MOOE  of  the
   MDRRMO.

The aforementioned audit observation  was  communicated  to  the  Management
thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-01 dated March 9, 2022.

Recommendations

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:


a. Direct the Inventory Committee  to  cause  the  performance  of  periodic
   physical inventory of supplies pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual
   for LGUs. The discrepancy between the inventory report and the accounting
   records should be investigated, reconciled, and adjusted; and


b. Require the preparation and submission of  the  Report  of  the  Physical
   Count of Inventories (RPCI). This shall be submitted to  the  Audit  Team
   not later than
   July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and  second  semesters,
   respectively.


The aforementioned audit observation  was  communicated  to  the  Management
thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-05 dated April 26, 2022.

Recommendation

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed  to  direct  the  OIC  –
Municipal Accountant to set up and maintain complete subsidiary records  for
each  special  account,  and  prepare  accordingly  the  trial  balance  and
financial statements for each special account in the  General  Fund  at  the
end of each year.

The aforementioned audit observation  was  communicated  to  the  Management
thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-02 dated March 9, 2022.

Recommendation

We recommended  and  the  Local  Chief  Executive  agreed  to  instruct  the
Municipal Engineering Office to coordinate  with  the  Municipal  Accounting
Office and devise a more systematic way of processing the GPPA documents  so
that the reports related thereto can be prepared  accurately  and  submitted
on time.

The aforementioned audit observation  was  communicated  to  the  Management
thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2022-03 dated April 12, 2022.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Direct the OIC – Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to cause the reconciliation of the results of actual physical count with the property and accounting records pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the COA Circular           No. 2020-006. The discrepancy between the inventory report and the accounting records should be investigated, reconciled, and adjusted;
Require the preparation and submission of the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to report all unserviceable property for disposal;
Direct the concerned personnel to exert effort in locating missing properties and furnish documents evidencing the disposal of properties to effect the dropping and derecognition of these disposed properties from the books of accounts; and
Direct the concerned personnel to verify the status of the breeding stocks and determine the extent of liability of the persons responsible for the said assets.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2021-01 dated February 17, 2021.

Recommendations
We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to record purchases of supplies and materials in their respective inventory accounts and take up expenses upon distribution and consumption following the provisions in the IPSAS 12.
We also recommended and the Management concurred that Supplies Ledger Cards per stock be maintained to ensure proper recording and updating of inventory record of stocks.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2021-06 dated March 17, 2021.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that all future donations during the implementation of community quarantine or state of calamity, whether in cash or in kind, are properly received/acknowledged, recorded, accounted for, utilized, distributed, and reported without necessarily disregarding basic controls and issuances governing donations or aids during a disaster or pandemic.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2021-05 dated March 17, 2021.

Recommendations
We recommended and the General Services Office agreed to maintain a repair and maintenance record/history of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or operated by the Municipality to avoid unnecessary repair and maintenance expenses. We suggested that a repair history form be devised, indicating the type of repair undertaken, the date when it was made, and other pertinent data for the information and guidance of the COA, the agency, and others concerned.
We further recommended and the General Services and Accounting Offices agreed to attach the repair and maintenance history/record to disbursement vouchers for repairs and maintenance expenses as supporting documents.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2021-03 dated February 24, 2021.

We recommended and the GSO agreed to prepare and submit regularly the monthly fuel consumption reports to the Accounting Office and the Auditor so that expenses on fuel, oil, and lubricants could be taken up in the books in a timely manner following the principle of matching, and so as to safeguard economical and efficient use of said petroleum products.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2021-06 dated March 17, 2021.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require all the department heads concerned to prepare, and the Municipal Engineer to consolidate and submit, at the beginning of the year, the list of all ongoing government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year, together with the Consolidated Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities for the quarter ended December 31, 2020, and years thereafter, to ensure compliance with laws, rules, and regulations, particularly COA Circular No. 2013-004.
We also recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to inform the Audit Team that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices are already posted for validation within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to the Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2021-02 dated February 17, 2021.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the PRAISE Committee to establish mechanisms for identifying, rewarding and providing incentives to deserving employees at the start of the year; Require the Officers and Employees of the Municipality of Tubungan to refund the monetary incentive given as the requirements for the grant of monetary awards were not met, hence resulting in an illegal expenditure.
observations.managementAction: The monetary incentive was not yet refunded. Audit finding is under appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the refund of the excess amount of PEI for CY 2013 totaling P1,200,000.00 paid to officials and personnel.
observations.managementAction: The monetary incentive was not yet refunded. Decision on the appeal was elevated to the Commission Proper.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  direct  the  GSO  to  exhaust
efforts and measures to submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of  each
year, pursuant to Chapter 7 of the Manual on the NGAS for  LGUs,  Volume  I,
so that the PPE accounts could be verified in a timely manner.

We recommended that in preparing the LDRRMIP, the  PPAs  should  be  result-
oriented.    Expected    Output/Performance     Indicators     should     be
quantifiable/measurable to ensure that the success or failure  of  the  PPAs
of the said Plan could be clearly established to rationalize the  allocation
and utilization of resources.

We also recommended that the items in the LDRRMIP be properly clustered
into four thematic areas pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  direct  the  MDRRMO  Head  to
utilize the 70% of the LDRRMF for  projects  and  activities  enumerated  in
NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.  For other operational  needs  of  the
MDRRMO, adequate funds from the General Fund should be appropriated for  the
purpose.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive convene with the Local  School
Board and prepare the three-year program for  the  strategic  prioritization
of policies in the allocation and use of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  the  Municipal  Budget
Officer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that approved SB  Resolutions
are secured prior to processing of payments for projects appropriated  under
programs/projects/activities stated in generic terms in  the  Annual  Budget
and Annual Procurement Plan (APP).

We also recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  require  the  Local
Development Council and  concerned  offices  to  be  more  specific  in  the
identification of priority projects and programs to be charged  against  the
20% MDF such that the projects included in the APP  and  the  Annual  Budget
already contain sufficient details and costing.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  the  OIC  -  Municipal
Accountant to make the adjusting entries and reflect the correct amounts  of
receivables for CY 2019.

Likewise, the OIC - Municipal Accountant need not close the  ending  balance
of receivables and deferred income accounts at the end of the year in  order
to  include  in  the  financial  statements  the  prior  years’  uncollected
amounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


     a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to  enhance  collection  efforts  so
        that RPT and SET Receivables are realized and tax delinquencies are
        collected; and


     b. Direct the Municipal Assessor  to  conduct  actual  inspection  and
        inventory of real properties in the  Municipality  so  that  proper
        classification, appraisal, and assessment can be made, and  a  more
        attainable target of RPT and SET Receivables can be set.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and concerned officials:

a. Incorporate in the contract,  MOA,  or  job  order  the  services  to  be
   rendered, the required number of man-hours, and quantity and duration  of
   the works to be performed by the job order personnel and require them  to
   submit quantified accomplishment reports; and

b. In  coordination  with  the  department  heads,  assess  and  review  the
   necessity of hiring job orders by considering the  existence  of  regular
   employees who could perform  some  of  the  contracted  works  or  tasks.
   Consider  maximizing  the  capabilities  and  services  of  the   regular
   employees to minimize the hiring of job order personnel.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  OIC  -  Municipal
Accountant  and  the  concerned  department  heads  to   submit   the   duly
accomplished AAPSI on each finding and recommendation discussed  in  the  CY
2018 Annual Audit Report pertinent to  their  offices,  in  compliance  with
Section 93 of the General Provisions  of  RA          No.  10964,  otherwise
known as the General Appropriations Act of 2018.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections to the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day, pursuant to the provisions of Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The LCE has directed the Municipal Treasurer to deposit daily but, considering the distance of the Municipality to the bank, deposits will be made as often as necessary as long as there is no collection on hand at the end of the week.

observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan has given the LCE an authority, through a resolution, to apply for a special patent of the untitled lot of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the Sangguniang Bayan, hire additional qualified personnel to occupy the two staff positions to assist the LDRRMO following the position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6 Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121 and Item 5.2, 5.3 and 5.4 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 to be able to perform their functions and responsibilities.

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-LDRRMO to ensure optimum utilization of the LDRRMF through proper planning, prioritization, and fast-tracking the implementation of priority projects, programs, and activities to afford its constituents the readiness of resources in times of disaster.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to formulate written policies/guidelines as to who can qualify (income bracket), what type of financial assistance to be given, the circumstances when to grant assistance and the amount of grant in order to prevent subjective disbursement of government funds pursuant to Section 2 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly comply with R.A. No. 9184 and its implementing rules and regulations. Otherwise, proper sanctions will be imposed due to non-compliance to the said regulations. We likewise recommended that disbursements be paid directly to suppliers so that taxes are withheld and deducted to provide the government of additional revenue.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the members of the GAD Focal Point System to comply with the provisions of PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA JMC 2013-01 as updated by JMC 2016-01 and particularly on the procedures to be followed in the formulation, development, submission, implementation, monitoring and evaluation including accounting of results of agency annual GAD Plans and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report in order to mainstream gender perspectives in their policies, program and projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all officials concerned to ensure optimal utilization of 20% Development Fund (current and continuing appropriation) through proper prioritization and fast-tracking the implementation of development projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the benefit of its constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Require the Officers and Employees of the Municipality of Tubungan to refund the monetary incentive given as the requirements for the grant of monetary awards were not met, hence resulting to an illegal expenditure.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the   Municipal
Treasurer to deposit all  collections  to  the  authorized  depository  bank
daily or  not  later  than  the  next  banking  day  as  prescribed  in  the
provisions of Section 69 of PD 1445  and  Section  32  of  MNGAS  for  LGUs,
Volume I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  the OIC  -  Municipal
Accountant to:


1. Make the following adjusting entries:
|Due from LGUs       |300,000.00           |                     |
|Due to LGUs         |                     |300,000.00           |
|To record the fund transfer to Brgy. Victoria, Tubungan        |

2. Monitor and demand the liquidation reports from the recipient LGUs;
3. Comply with the requirements provided in COA Circular  No.  2012-001  and
   other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  OIC-Municipal
Accountant to:


a. Implement the prompt and proper liquidation of trust funds received  from
   Source Agencies, including the return of excess/unutilized funds.


b. Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers with the  Source  Agencies  and
   prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  the General  Services
Officer to prepare the Inventory  and  Inspection  Report  of  Unserviceable
Property  (IIRUP)  and  undertake   proper   and   necessary   disposal   of
unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445.


We further recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  and  all  officials
concerned exert effort in locating missing properties and furnish  documents
evidencing the disposal of  properties  to  effect  the  dropping  of  these
disposed properties from the books of accounts.


We also recommended  that  the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  derecognized  the
carrying amounts of the unserviceable properties after the disposal  and  to
recognize the gain or loss arising therefrom.

We recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  instruct  the  concerned
personnel to secure supporting documents  evidencing  the  donation  of  the
ambulance unit.


We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal  Accountant  make  the  necessary
adjusting entry to record the cost of ambulance unit based on the  appraised
cost to be determined by the Appraisal Committee.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the   proponent
department/office that  any  projects  implemented  chargeable  against  the
Local Disaster Risk Reduction  Management  Fund  should  be  supported  with
statistics, studies on the choice of projects with  objectives  specifically
identifying  the  risk,  hazard,  vulnerabilities  and  negative  impact  of
calamity being mitigated/prevented as well as  the  affected  locations  and
people. Likewise, proper  monitoring  and  post-evaluation  of  the  project
implemented  should  be  done  in  order   to   assess   the   success   and
responsiveness of the  LDRRMF  projects  to  prevent  and  mitigate  adverse
impact of the natural hazard, calamity, vulnerabilities and  climate  change
risk.

adjusting entry:

|To correct recording of the cost and amount collected from selling of  |
|NFA rice                                                               |

funds will be set forth in the revised AIP and will be in accordance with
the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, Sangguniang Bayan  and  other
officials concerned appropriate one percent (1%)  of  its  Internal  Revenue
Allotment in the Annual Budget for the strengthening and  implementation  of
programs, projects and activities of Local Council for the Protection  of
Children (LCPC) in order to cater  the  needs  and  uphold  the  rights  and
interests of the children in the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct  the  Municipal  Social
Welfare and Development Officer to formulate written policies/guidelines  as
to who can qualify (income bracket), what type of  financial  assistance  to
be given, the circumstances when to  grant  assistance  and  the  amount  of
grant in order  to  prevent  subjective  disbursement  of  government  funds

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids  and  Awards
Committee to strictly comply with R.A. No. 9184 and its  implementing  rules

compliance to the said regulations.


We likewise recommended that disbursements be paid directly to suppliers  so
that  taxes  are  withheld  and  deducted  to  provide  the  government   of
additional revenue.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the members of the  GAD

2013-01 as updated by JMC 2016-01 and particularly on the procedures  to  be

gender perspectives in their policies, programs and projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide the corresponding allowance for depreciation for depreciable properties valued at P48,094,111.90 in compliance to existing government rules and regulations.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant make the following adjusting entries: GF Books: Dr. - Breeding Stocks Cr. - Government Equity Amount : P2,063,800.00 To record transfer of Breeding Stocks to GF Books Dr. - Other Receivables Cr. - Breeding Stocks Amount : P2,063,800.00 To record distribution of Breeding Stocks to identified beneficiaries We also recommended that subsidiary ledgers for each beneficiary be maintained to properly monitor the payments and to easily determine the unpaid balance at any given time.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: Transfer the fund from General Fund to Trust Fund; Adhere strictly with the provisions of General Appropriations Act of 2016, COA Circular No. 94-013 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and concerned officials and employees: Stop the practice of charging expenditures against 20% Development Fund that do not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Trust Liability – DRRM account to determine the sources as to which year or who donated the fund pursuant to Section 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and to identify the amount that can be transferred from Special Trust Fund to General Fund for Social Services at the lapse of five years.

We further recommended the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to monitor the 10- Year Solid Waste Management Plan as required in Section 12(3) of RA 9003 so that projects will be implemented in accordance with desired schedule for the betterment of the constituents.

Section 2 of IRR of RA No.  10121  and  Item  5.2,  5.3  and  5.4  of  Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 to be  able  to  properly
perform their functions and responsibilities as LDRRM Officer and Staff.

We recommended that  the  LDRRMO  Designate  and  OIC  Municipal  Accountant
adhere strictly with the  provisions  of  Item  5.1.5,  and  5.1.12  of  COA
Circular  No.  2012-002  dated  September  12,  2012  so  that  the   proper
utilization, recording and releases  of  the  Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and
Management Fund can be verified in a timely manner and that proper  charging
and recording can be effected.

We also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  maintain  subsidiary
ledgers for the Trust Liability – DRRM account to determine the  sources  as
to which year or who donated the fund pursuant  to  Section  5.1.12  of  COA
Circular No.2012-002 and to identify the amount  that  can  be  transferred
from Special Trust Fund to General Fund for Social Services at the lapse  of
five years.

We further  recommended  that  the  LDRRMO  Designate  strictly  follow  the
guidelines set in Section 6.5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint  Memorandum  Circular
No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 so  that  the  release  and  utilization  of
funds will be set forth in the revised AIP and will be  in  accordance  with
the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan of the municipality.

Recommendations

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal  Solid  Waste
Management Board to formulate  and  adopt  revenue-generating  measures  for
approval and proper resolution of the Municipal Sangguniang  Bayan  pursuant
to Section 12 (4) of RA 9003 in order to ensure the  viability  of  its  10-
Year Solid Waste Management Plan.

We also recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of DILG-
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in  charging  expenditures  against
20% Development Fund and to  formulate  projects,  programs  and  activities
that partake the nature of capital investment and has long term benefits  to
the constituents.

We further  recommended  the  Municipal  Solid  Waste  Management  Board  to
monitor the 10- Year Solid Waste Management  Plan  as  required  in  Section
12(3) of RA 9003 so that projects will be  implemented  in  accordance  with
desired schedule for the betterment of the constituents.

Recommendation

We   recommended   that   management   comply   with   the   provisions   of
PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA JMC 2013-01 as updated by JMC 2016-01 and particularly  on
the procedures to be followed in the formulation,  development,  submission,
implementation, monitoring and evaluation including  accounting  of  results
of agency annual GAD Plans and Budget and GAD  AR  in  order  to  mainstream
gender perspectives in their policies, programs and projects.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  require  the  concerned
officials to strictly observe the  provisions  of  COA  Circular  No.  75-6,
dated November 7,  1975  and  the  Manual  for  Audit  of  Fuel  Consumption
prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61, dated September 26,  1977,  on  the
submission  of  mandatory  reports,  relative  to  the  use  of   government
vehicles.

We recommend that the Inventory Committee conduct complete physical inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment and prepare a report thereon. In the absence of the General Services Office, the Treasurer’s Office and the Accounting Office each assign personnel to maintain separate ledger cards for all property and to work on the reconciliation of the accounting and property records with the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment in order to establish correctness of the PPE accounts.

We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Accountant provide every property and equipment with corresponding allowance for depreciation based on the formula as provided for by existing rules and regulations to reflect the true and correct balance of the account in the financial statements.
Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and all officials and employees concerned exert effort to fully implement the programs and projects under the 20% Development Fund in order that the constituents will enjoy optimum benefits out of these projects and to enhance the general welfare of the populace. The Annual Development/Investment Plan must be drawn in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Council who shall determine the nature of the programs/projects that should be prioritized so that accomplishment thereof will be made efficiently and effectively.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require all concerned officers and employees to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances. Cash advances for travel should be liquidated within thirty (30) days after completion of the said travel.
We also recommend the immediate refund of all unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, withhold the salaries of all officers and employees with outstanding cash advances until the amounts involved are fully restituted.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 75-6, dated November 7, 1975 and the Manual for Audit of Fuel Consumption prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61, dated September 26, 1977, on the submission of mandatory reports, relative to the use of government vehicles.

We recommend that the remaining PDAF be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund (GF) pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2013-008 dated December 6, 2013 applying the prescribed rules on fund transfer from Trust Fund, where these remaining balances are booked, to the GF. Subsequently, these funds should be remitted or returned to the Department of Finance thru the Bureau of Treasury.
